benchmark_details.name: HalluDial (A Large-Scale Benchmark for Automatic Dialogue-Level Hallucination Evaluation)
benchmark_details.overview: HalluDial is the first comprehensive large-scale benchmark for automatic dialogue-level hallucination evaluation, covering both spontaneous and induced hallucination scenarios, including factuality and faithfulness hallucinations with a total of 146,856 samples.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To study and evaluate hallucinations of large language models in information-seeking dialogues.
purpose_and_intended_users.limitations: While the HalluDial dataset is relatively large, it may not fully capture all real-world conversation scenarios.

data.source: Derived from an information-seeking dialogue dataset.

methodology.calculation: Metrics are calculated based on hallucination detection tasks using evaluators like HalluJudge and other language models.
methodology.interpretation: A higher F1 score indicates better performance in detecting hallucinations.
methodology.baseline_results: HalluJudge achieved an F1 Score of 84.92.
methodology.validation: Validation through performance comparison with established LLMs.

targeted_risks.harm: May contain potentially sensitive topics related to big events.

ethical_and_legal_considerations.privacy_and_anonymity: All dialogues are sourced from publicly available data, minimizing privacy risks.
